parts.title: The Map
parts.text: Captain Lena was **certain** she had found something important beneath the old floorboards. Wrapped in cloth was a map with a red line forming the **border** of a mysterious island. She knew she had to **depart** before the tide changed.

parts.title: The Voyage
parts.text: The **journey** across the sea lasted nine days. On the fourth night, waves turned **fierce** and pounded the hull without mercy. But the wind soon became **swift**, and the ship cut through the water quickly. When she arrived, Lena began to **observe** the shoreline with care, looking for any sign of what the map promised.

parts.title: The Discovery
parts.text: At last, beneath a great tree, she found a chest of gold — a **treasure** so **precious** she could barely breathe looking at it. Inside was a letter urging all who found it to **unite** and share the wealth. Lena thought this was a **superb** plan. She used the **clasp** on her cloak to pin the letter to her chest, then turned home — her heart full.

parts.title: The Return
parts.text: When Lena returned to her village, she called everyone together and read the letter aloud. The villagers agreed to share the treasure equally. They used the gold to build a new school, a bridge, and a lighthouse. Lena's name was carved into the lighthouse wall, and sailors remembered her as the captain who brought a village together.

definitions.meaning1: Of great value; not to be wasted or treated carelessly. Example: Clean water is precious to villagers in the desert.
definitions.meaning2: Greatly loved or treasured. Example: She kept her grandmother's ring because it was precious to her.
definitions.studentChallenge: The ___ diamond was kept in a locked glass case at the museum.

definitions.meaning1: Moving or happening very quickly. Example: The runner was so swift that she crossed the finish line first.
definitions.studentChallenge: We had to make a ___ decision before the timer ran out.

definitions.meaning1: To join together as one. Example: The two rival towns decided to unite and build one strong community.
definitions.studentChallenge: The teacher asked the students to ___ their voices and sing together.

definitions.meaning1: The edge or boundary of something. Example: The fence marked the border between our yard and the neighbor's garden.
definitions.meaning2: A line separating two countries or regions. Example: They crossed the border into the next country.
definitions.studentChallenge: A bright red line formed the ___ around the map, showing where one country ended.

definitions.meaning1: Sure; having no doubt. Example: She was certain that she had left her backpack on the bus.
definitions.meaning2: Specific but not named. Example: Certain students were chosen to lead the project.
definitions.studentChallenge: One thing is ___: if you practice every day, you will improve.

definitions.meaning1: To hold something tightly. Example: She clasped her hand tightly so they wouldn't get separated in the crowd.
definitions.meaning2: A device for fastening things together. Example: He used a silver clasp to hold his cape in place.
definitions.studentChallenge: Mom told me to ___ her hand tightly as we crossed the busy street.

definitions.meaning1: To leave a place. Example: The train was scheduled to depart at noon.
definitions.studentChallenge: It was hard to watch the ship ___ because our friends wouldn't return until spring.

definitions.meaning1: Very strong, violent, or intense. Example: The wolf let out a fierce growl that silenced every animal.
definitions.meaning2: Showing strong feelings or determination. Example: She had a fierce desire to win the championship.
definitions.studentChallenge: A ___ storm knocked down three oak trees overnight.

definitions.meaning1: A long trip from one place to another. Example: The explorer's journey through the jungle took three weeks.
definitions.meaning2: A process of personal change. Example: Growing up is a long journey with many surprising turns.
definitions.studentChallenge: The family's ___ across the country took five days by car.

definitions.meaning1: To watch carefully. Example: Scientists went to the rainforest to observe how the rare butterflies behaved.
definitions.meaning2: To follow or obey a rule or custom. Example: Many families observe the tradition of eating together on Sundays.
definitions.studentChallenge: From the back of the classroom, she would quietly ___ the new student.

definitions.meaning1: Excellent; of the highest quality. Example: The gymnast gave a superb performance, earning a perfect score.
definitions.studentChallenge: The meal was ___ — the flavors were balanced and everything was fresh.

definitions.meaning1: A collection of valuable things like gold or jewels. Example: Pirates searched for buried treasure beneath the old lighthouse.
definitions.meaning2: To value something greatly. Example: He chose to treasure the memory of his dog by keeping a photo on his desk.
definitions.studentChallenge: The explorers finally found the hidden ___ inside the ancient cave.

doubleTakeQuiz.sentence1: The runner was so _______ that she crossed the finish line before anyone else even blinked.
doubleTakeQuiz.sentence2: We had to make a _______ decision before the timer ran out, with no time to think.

doubleTakeQuiz.sentence1: Scientists went to the rainforest to _______ how the rare butterflies behaved in the wild.
doubleTakeQuiz.sentence2: From the back of the classroom, Marcus would quietly _______ the new student to see if she needed a friend.

doubleTakeQuiz.sentence1: She kept her grandmother's ring in a velvet box because it was too _______ to risk losing.
doubleTakeQuiz.sentence2: Clean water is _______ to villagers in the desert, where every drop must be saved.

doubleTakeQuiz.sentence1: The two rival towns decided to _______ and build one strong community together.
doubleTakeQuiz.sentence2: The teacher asked the students to _______ their voices and sing as one choir.

doubleTakeQuiz.sentence1: The explorer's _______ through the jungle took three weeks and crossed four rivers.
doubleTakeQuiz.sentence2: Growing up is a long _______ with many surprising turns along the way.

doubleTakeQuiz.sentence1: Mom told me to _______ her hand tightly so we wouldn't get separated in the crowd.
doubleTakeQuiz.sentence2: He used a silver _______ to hold his cape in place at the costume parade.

doubleTakeQuiz.sentence1: The wolf let out a _______ growl that made every animal in the forest go completely silent.
doubleTakeQuiz.sentence2: A _______ storm knocked down three oak trees and flooded the basement overnight.

doubleTakeQuiz.sentence1: The train was scheduled to _______ at noon, so we rushed to reach the platform in time.
doubleTakeQuiz.sentence2: It was hard to watch the ship _______ because we knew our friends wouldn't return until spring.

doubleTakeQuiz.sentence1: She was _______ that she had left her backpack on the bus — she remembered it clearly.
doubleTakeQuiz.sentence2: One thing is _______: if you practice every day, you will improve.

doubleTakeQuiz.sentence1: The gymnast gave a _______ performance, earning a perfect score from every single judge.
doubleTakeQuiz.sentence2: The meal was _______ — the flavors were balanced, the bread was warm, and everything was fresh.

doubleTakeQuiz.sentence1: The fence marked the _______ between our yard and the neighbor's garden.
doubleTakeQuiz.sentence2: A bright red line formed the _______ around the map, showing where one country ended and another began.

doubleTakeQuiz.sentence1: He chose to _______ the memory of his dog by keeping a photo on his desk always.
doubleTakeQuiz.sentence2: Pirates searched for buried _______ hidden beneath the old lighthouse on the rocky shore.

secretPassage.instructions: Read the passage below. Fill in each blank with the correct vocabulary word from the word bank.
secretPassage.passage:
Captain Lena was _(1)_ she had found something important beneath the old floorboards. Wrapped in cloth was a map with a red line forming the _(2)_ of a mysterious island. She knew she had to _(3)_ before the tide changed.

The _(4)_ across the sea lasted nine days. On the fourth night, waves turned _(5)_ and pounded the hull without mercy. But the wind soon became _(6)_, and the ship cut through the water quickly.

When she arrived, Lena began to _(7)_ the shoreline with care, looking for any sign of what the map promised. At last, beneath a great tree, she found a chest of gold — a _(8)_ so _(9)_ she could barely breathe looking at it. Inside was a letter urging all who found it to _(10)_ and share the wealth. Lena thought this was a _(11)_ plan. She used the _(12)_ on her cloak to pin the letter to her chest, then turned home — her heart full.

wordScaleImposter.studentPick: Pick 2 imposters and explain why they are opposites of the vocabulary word.
